name: Serra de Bornes Loop
name_original: Circular por el Rio Tua, Sambade e Vilarelhos — desde Trindade
description: This difficult 46.7-mile road cycling route from Trindade offers challenging climbs, river views, and passes through Sambade and Vilarelhos.
region_intro: Road cycling around Trindade offers diverse landscapes, from lush mountains to the Atlantic Forest and coastal areas. The region is characterized by significant elevation changes, providing challenging ascents and scenic descents. Cyclists can navigate routes that traverse both forested terrain and open stretches, with views of the surrounding natural features. The area's geography includes a mix of mountainous backdrops and proximity to the sea, influencing the varied road cycling experiences available. Best road cycling routes around Trindade The most popular road cycling route is Serra de Bornes Loop, a 46.7 miles (75.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 12 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Escarpa da Falha da Vilariça e Albufeira de Sambade — circular desde Sambade, a moderate 16.1 miles (26.0 km) path. This route includes notable climbs and descents, offering views of the landscape. Local road cyclists also love the Estevinha Reservoir and Alfândega da Fé Loop, a 12.0 miles (19.3 km) trail leading through reservoir areas and local surroundings, often completed in about 1 hour. Road cycling around Trindade is defined by lush mountains, Atlantic Forest, and coastal influences. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to challenging climbs. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 10 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Trindade's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Trindade?
A: There are over 5 distinct road cycling routes around Trindade, offering a variety of experiences from easy loops to challenging climbs through the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy ride?
A: Yes, Trindade offers at least one easy road cycling route. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1405783203/estevinha-reservoir-and-alfandega-da-fe-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estevinha Reservoir and Alfândega da Fé Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 12.0-mile (19.3 km) trail that takes approximately 1 hour to complete, featuring minimal elevation gain and leading through reservoir areas and local surroundings.

Q: What kind of challenges can I expect on road cycling routes in Trindade?
A: Road cycling around Trindade is characterized by significant elevation changes, with many routes featuring substantial climbs and descents. Six of the available routes are rated as difficult, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1403257453/serra-de-bornes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra de Bornes Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 46.7 miles (75.2 km) with over 1600 meters of elevation gain, traversing varied and demanding terrain.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Trindade?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1403270873/escarpa-da-falha-da-vilarica-e-albufeira-de-sambade-circular-desde-sambade" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Escarpa da Falha da Vilariça e Albufeira de Sambade — circular desde Sambade&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 16.1-mile (26.0 km) path that offers notable climbs and descents in a circular format. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1403283742/vilarelhos-is-near-lagos-do-sabor-circular-from-alfandega-da-fe" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vilarelhos is near Lagos do Sabor — circular from Alfândega da Fé&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 27.9-mile (44.9 km) route.

Q: What are the best times of year for road biking in Trindade?
A: Trindade's coastal and mountainous climate generally offers pleasant conditions for road cycling for much of the year. The region's lush Atlantic Forest and proximity to the sea mean temperatures are often mild. However, it's always advisable to check local weather forecasts, especially during the rainy season, to ensure optimal riding conditions.

Q: What kind of scenic views can I expect on road cycling routes in Trindade?
A: Road cycling routes in Trindade offer breathtaking panoramas, blending rainforest-covered mountains with stunning coastal vistas. You'll experience diverse landscapes, from lush Atlantic Forest and mountainous backdrops to glimpses of the sea and natural features like rivers and reservoirs. The journey from Paraty to Trindade, for instance, is known for its spectacular coastal and mountain views.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions to see along the road cycling routes?
A: While cycling, you can explore various points of interest. Nearby attractions include the settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6343504" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirandela&lt;/a&gt;, the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7633602" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alameda do Rio Tua, Mirandela&lt;/a&gt;, and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6346289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of the Assumption&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also pass through areas with trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6351952" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Descent to Santa Comba de Vilariça&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that offer a mix of coastal and mountainous terrain?
A: Yes, the region is uniquely positioned between mountains and the sea. The journey from Paraty to Trindade, for example, is a well-known route that traverses both coastal and mountainous landscapes, offering a challenging ride with significant elevation gain and diverse scenery.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Trindade?
A: The komoot community frequently praises the diverse landscapes and challenging terrain around Trindade. Cyclists particularly enjoy the blend of lush mountains, the Atlantic Forest, and coastal influences, which provide both scenic beauty and engaging rides. The variety of routes caters to different skill levels, making it appealing for many.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by reservoirs or significant water bodies?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate views of local water bodies. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1405783203/estevinha-reservoir-and-alfandega-da-fe-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estevinha Reservoir and Alfândega da Fé Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, leads through reservoir areas, offering pleasant views and a relatively easy ride.

Q: Can I find routes that are longer for endurance training?
A: For endurance training, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1403257453/serra-de-bornes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra de Bornes Loop&lt;/a&gt; are ideal. This difficult route spans 46.7 miles (75.2 km) and involves over 1600 meters of elevation gain, providing a substantial challenge for experienced road cyclists.
tips: Just north of Albufeira de Santa Justa and this land of olive trees with small hills appears this beautiful town called Vilarelhos.
Small town with beautiful surroundings. Basically what is called the Falha da Vilariça Escarpment, to the north of the town.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Circular by the Rio Tua, Sambade and Vilarelhos — from Trindade route?
faqs.answer: This is a challenging road cycling route, classified as 'difficult'. It requires a very good level of fitness due to its significant elevation gain of 1,600 meters over 75.2 kilometers. Expect a ride lasting approximately 4 hours and 12 minutes.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of mostly well-paved surfaces that are easy to ride. However, be aware that some segments of the route may be unpaved and difficult, potentially requiring you to push your bike for short sections.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers beautiful surroundings, including river views and terraced vineyards typical of the region. You'll pass through settlements like Vilarelhos and Sambade, a small town known for its beautiful surroundings and proximity to the Falha da Vilariça Escarpment. While specific points of interest aren't detailed, the region often features charming cafes and historic towns.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the route from Trindade?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Trindade. While specific parking details for this exact starting point are not provided, it's generally advisable to look for public parking areas within or near the village center. Given it's a circular route, you'll finish where you started.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to the starting point in Trindade?
faqs.answer: Information on direct public transport to Trindade for this specific cycling route is limited. It's recommended to check local bus schedules or regional transport options if you plan to rely on public transport to reach the starting point.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Cycling in Portugal generally offers pleasant conditions outside of the peak summer heat. Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) are often ideal, providing milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as 'difficult' and requires 'very good fitness'. It is not suitable for beginner road cyclists due to its significant length and elevation gain. Experienced cyclists seeking a strenuous workout will find it more appropriate.

faqs.question: Is the Circular by the Rio Tua, Sambade and Vilarelhos — from Trindade route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, bringing a dog on a demanding 75 km road cycling route is not typically recommended or practical. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it can safely and comfortably accompany you for the entire duration, perhaps in a specialized bike trailer.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Circular by the Rio Tua, Sambade and Vilarelhos — from Trindade route, as it primarily uses public roads and paths.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay along the route?
faqs.answer: While specific establishments aren't detailed, cycling in this region of Portugal often presents opportunities to encounter charming cafes and local restaurants in towns like Sambade and Vilarelhos. For accommodation, you would likely find options in these settlements or nearby larger towns.

faqs.question: Can I download a GPX file for this route?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can download the GPX file for the Circular by the Rio Tua, Sambade and Vilarelhos — from Trindade route directly from its Komoot tour page to navigate offline. This is highly recommended for such a demanding route.
